JavaScript Core Concepts (48) — Inheritance & Prototype Chain: Where Does the Prototype Live?

Starting this chapter we finally get into prototypes for real. We'll look at why JavaScript objects inherit from prototypes, what the prototype chain actually is, how methods get shared across instances, and why you should never add methods through __proto__ in real code.
JavaScript Core Concepts (47): Why Does JavaScript Even Have Prototypes?

Time to dig into what a prototype actually is in JavaScript. We'll start with a bit of history — why JavaScript got named the way it did, how it relates to Java, and why that history is the reason JavaScript uses prototypal inheritance instead of class-based inheritance. By the end you'll understand where prototypes come from and why the language needs them.
Learn Vim in 10 Minutes With VSCode (Beginner Walkthrough)
A beginner walkthrough for Vim — fair warning, you'll definitely hit walls early on. The concept is honestly closer to learning a new input method than a new editor.
JavaScript Core Concepts (46): Functions and How This Works — Practice Quiz

Time for another round of practice questions. This chapter is all about functions and how `this` works — expressions vs statements, IIFEs, closures, and the classic `this` gotchas. Ten quiz questions with full answers and explanations so you can check whether the concepts actually stuck.
JavaScript Core (45) - Functions & This: The Classic Trick Questions

This chapter pulls together a bunch of classic function trick questions in JavaScript. We'll walk through why `this` ends up pointing at the global object, how `bind` quietly eats one of your arguments, the assignment- expression gotcha, and the famous `['1', '2', '3'].map(parseInt)` puzzle.
JavaScript Core (44) — Functions and How This Works: this Practice Problems

Time to drill on this. This post is a set of ten quick quiz questions about how this gets bound in JavaScript — each with a code snippet, multiple choices, and a hidden answer with an explanation. Think it through before you peek.
JavaScript Core Concepts (43) - Functions and How This Works - this and the DOM

Let's look at how this behaves when you're working with the DOM. Whether you bind a handler inline in your HTML or with addEventListener, this points at the element itself - which is a classic gotcha. And if you want it pointing somewhere else, bind is the tool to reach for.
JavaScript Core (42) — Functions and How This Works: call, apply, bind, and Strict Mode

This time we get into some of the more interesting corners of this — namely call, apply, and bind. These three look almost identical, which is exactly why people mix them up. I'll walk through how each one redirects this, where they differ, and then finish with what strict mode actually does for this.
JavaScript Core (41) — Functions and This: The Simple Call

This one picks up where the last chapter left off and covers the "Simple Call" — what it is, the situations that trigger it, and why it always sends `this` flying off to `window`. We'll walk through IIFEs, closures, callbacks like setTimeout and forEach, and the classic trick of stashing `this` in a variable so it doesn't get lost.

Seen people show off their GitHub profile pages and wondered how to do that? This post is the quick tour — creating the special username-named repo with a README that becomes your profile, plus using the github-readme-stats project to add stats cards, theme them, and show your top languages.
